title: dryopteris marginalis var. elegans
intro: Dryopteris marginalis var. elegans, also known as the wood fern, is a variety of the Dryopteris marginalis plant, a type of deciduous fern native to Eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The wood fern has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving respiratory function, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
